chapter_title.ar: باب السُّنَّةِ فِي لُبْسِ الْخُفِّ وَ النَّعْلِ وَ خَلْعِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 20 – The Sunnah regarding wearing of the socks, and the sandals, and taking them off

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/353)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/353)
text: مَنْ مَشَى فِي حِذَاءٍ وَاحِدٍ فَأَصَابَهُ مَسٌّ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ لَمْ يَدَعْهُ إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Al A’ala, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who walks in one shoe, so a touch of the Satan<sup>la</sup> hits him, it would not leave him (the injury) except what Allah<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires’.
en.sarwar: 5. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one walks with one shoe and if a touch of Satan reaches, him it will not leave him unless Allah wants otherwise.’”
